sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Real Madrid have shown strong attacking form in their last 5 matches, scoring 11 goals despite a heavy 4-0 loss to PSG. Osasuna, on the other hand, have struggled, managing only 4 goals in their last 5 games with inconsistent results. Real Madrid’s dominance in possession and shot creation stands out.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two encounters, Real Madrid have been dominant, securing a 4-0 win at home and a 1-1 draw away against Osasuna. Real Madrid have not lost to Osasuna in their last 20 meetings, often scoring first and controlling the game.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: Kylian Mbappé has been in exceptional form for Real Madrid, scoring 5 goals in the last 5 matches, while Vinícius Júnior has historically performed well against Osasuna. For Osasuna, Ante Budimir is the key threat with 3 goals recently, but their overall attack lacks consistency.

sections.subtitle: Statistical Insights
sections.text: Real Madrid’s games often see over 2.5 goals (5/6), and they frequently score first (8/9). Osasuna’s matches tend to have under 2.5 goals (7/9), and they often concede early. Both teams have low card counts, with less than 4.5 cards in recent games.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, the score remains goalless between Real Madrid and Osasuna at the Santiago Bernabéu. Real Madrid have dominated possession and territory, spending significantly more time in the attacking third compared to Osasuna. Despite their control, they haven't managed to convert their opportunities into goals, while Osasuna have struggled to create any meaningful threat in the final third. Both teams head into the second half with everything still to play for.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Real Madrid's first half performance
halftimeSections.text: Real Madrid have been the dominant force in the first 45 minutes, controlling the game with an overwhelming share of possession. Their ability to enter the final third and penetrate the penalty area has been notable, though they've lacked the cutting edge to finish their chances. Key players like Kylian Mbappé and Vinícius Júnior have been involved in creating opportunities, but the final touch has eluded them. Defensively, they've been solid, barely allowing Osasuna any space to operate in dangerous areas.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Osasuna's first half performance
halftimeSections.text: Osasuna have had a tough first half, spending most of their time on the back foot. Their limited possession and minimal entries into the final third reflect a defensive approach, focusing on containing Real Madrid's attacking waves. They've managed to hold firm at the back, with players like Alejandro Catena contributing to a resilient defensive structure. However, their lack of forward momentum has left them without a single shot on target, highlighting a clear struggle to transition into attack.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: Throughout the first half, Real Madrid consistently held the upper hand in terms of attacking momentum. From the opening minutes, they established a strong presence in Osasuna's half, with frequent spells of pressure that kept the away side pinned back. Their attacking intent peaked at several moments, showcasing sustained waves of pressure, particularly in the middle and latter stages of the half. Osasuna, in contrast, showed almost no forward momentum, with only a brief flicker of intent in added time, which quickly faded.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key areas to watch in the second half
halftimeSections.text: For Real Madrid, the focus will likely be on sharpening their finishing and capitalizing on their territorial dominance. Players like Mbappé, with his knack for finding space, could be pivotal if they exploit gaps in Osasuna's defense. On the other hand, Osasuna will need to take more risks if they hope to turn the tide, potentially pushing forward to create counter-attacking opportunities. Their defensive resilience will be tested further if Real Madrid maintain their relentless pressure.
